sources.title: Google to punish sites that trap people in with back button tricks
sources.summary: The tech giant said it will punish sites that block back button navigation from June.
sources.content_text: [Skip to content](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c05dd2yj3z3o#bbc-main) [Watch Live](https://www.bbc.com/watch-live-news/) [British Broadcasting Corporation](https://www.bbc.com/) Subscribe [Sign In](https://session.bbc.com/session?userOrigin=BBCS_BBC&ptrt=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.bbc.com%2Fnews%2Farticles%2Fc05dd2yj3z3o) * [Home](https://www.bbc.com/) * [News](https://www.bbc.com/news) * [Sport](https://www.bbc.com/sport) * [Business](https://www.bbc.com/business) * [Technology](https://www.bbc.com/technology) * [Health](https://www.bbc.com/health) * [Culture](https://www.bbc.com/culture) * [Arts](https://www.bbc.com/arts) * [Travel](https://www.bbc.com/travel) * [Earth](https://www.bbc.com/future-planet) * [Audio](https://www.bbc.com/audio) * [Video](https://www.bbc.com/video) * [Live](https://www.bbc.com/live) * [Documentaries](https://www.bbc.com/video/docs) [Home](https://www.bbc.com/) News [Sport](https://www.bbc.com/sport) Business Technology [Health](https://www.bbc.com/health) Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Documentaries [Weather](https://www.bbc.com/weather) [Newsletters](https://www.bbc.com/newsletters) [Watch Live](https://www.bbc.com/watch-live-news/) # Google to punish sites that trap people in with back button tricks 1 hour ago Share Save [Add as preferred on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=bbc.com) Laura CressTechnology reporter ![Getty Images A close up of a woman wearing a green blouse typing on a grey laptop](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/36f9/live/c3f2a8f0-38a7-11f1-bd3c-df67f5a99d2a.jpg.webp)Getty Images Google says it is expanding its policies to crack down on websites which trap users with "back button hijacking". Back button hijacking is when a website interferes with a browser so the back button no longer takes users to the previous page, instead often keeping them on the site or presenting unsolicited ads. In a blog post the tech giant behind the Chrome browser said it had seen a "rise of this type of behaviour" which had led it to act. From 15 June the tactic will be deemed a "malicious practice", meaning sites which continue to adopt it may be down-ranked or even removed from Google Search results. "Back button hijacking interferes with the browser's functionality, breaks the expected user journey, and results in user frustration," Google [said in its post](https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2026/04/back-button-hijacking). "People report feeling manipulated and eventually less willing to visit unfamiliar sites," it added. Examples of practices it would clamp down on included sites using any technique which inserted "manipulative" pages into a user's browser history that stopped them from returning to the previous page. Adam Thompson, director of digital at BCS, the Chartered Institute for IT, told the BBC: "Practices like back button hijacking undermine the basic user experience and break the expectations people have of how the web should work, so it's understandable that Google views this as a harmful behaviour and [is] taking action." Google advised site owners which did not want to face the new penalties to ensure they did not do "anything to interfere with a user's ability to navigate their browser history", urging them to "thoroughly review their technical implementation". It added sites which were penalised but then fixed the issue could submit a request to Google to have the demotion reconsidered. ## [Businesses scramble to get noticed by AI search](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c70n2rjgxeyo#:~:text=More%20Technology%20of%20Business%20*%2010%20March.,at%20festivals%20and%20sports%20matches.%2027%20February.) ## [Google launches new AI search feature in UK](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/clyj4zky4zwo) ## [Sell Chrome to end search monopoly, Google told](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cp81ppr3l9go) ![A green promotional banner with black squares and rectangles forming pixels, moving in from the right. The text says: “Tech Decoded: The world’s biggest tech news in your inbox every Monday.”](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/41d3/live/348b21e0-26a8-11f0-8f57-b7237f6a66e6.png.webp) [Sign up for our Tech Decoded newsletter](https://www.bbc.co.uk/newsletters/zxh6cxs) to follow the world's top tech stories and trends. [Outside the UK? Sign up here](https://cloud.email.bbc.com/techdecoded-newsletter-signup). Why?](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cde5y2x51y8o) ## ['We're having a moment' - fear and denial in Silicon Valley over social media addiction trial](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c86e3eglv2go) * * * More from the BBC [ 15 hrs ago ![](https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/bbcdotcom/web/20260407-092955-f3cfe0ee04-web-3.0.0-2/grey-placeholder.png)![A rocket taking off from a launchpad with the aim of putting an Amazon Leo satellite in low-earth orbit ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/8f91/live/1c6e9b40-383c-11f1-b164-cbd9e6dd0bb7.jpg.webp) Amazon to spend $11bn on satellite firm in growing Starlink rivalry Amazon wants to get thousands more satellites into orbit to offer internet and mobile services. 15 hrs ago ](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/clyjjr7kzj2o) [ 2 days ago ![](https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/bbcdotcom/web/20260407-092955-f3cfe0ee04-web-3.0.0-2/grey-placeholder.png)![A technician works on a undersea fibre optic cable at Arrietara beach near the Spanish Basque village of Sopelana](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/127b/live/0ffa6830-eecd-11ef-bd1b-d536627785f2.jpg.webp) What are the cables linking Somerset to New York? sources.title: Aux Etats-Unis, l’émergence d’un nouveau salarié dopé à l’IA
sources.summary: Mesurer la performance en tokens, sorte de « jetons d’IA », plutôt qu’en heures travaillées : dans la Silicon Valley, l’intelligence artificielle redéfinit les critères de valeur des employés. Avec, en creux, la menace d’un déclassement pour ceux qui restent à l’écart.

sources.title: « Les géants de la tech constituent les plus vastes agrégations privées de données de santé jamais envisagées »
sources.summary: Dans une tribune au « Monde », le professeur de droit du numérique à l’université Grenoble-Alpes Théodore Christakis examine les enjeux éthiques et juridiques liés à l’émergence de plateformes dévolues à la santé, pilotées par les géants de l’intelligence artificielle.
